title: Inventory heat-mortality, warning-system coverage, and forecast-based financing data sources for LMICs
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates heat-mortality data (vital registration, sentinel surveillance, burial records), warning-system coverage data (WMO, IFRC, national meteorological agencies), and forecast-based financing program data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC data-quality assessment.
inputs: WMO State of Climate Services reports
IFRC Climate Centre forecast-based financing documentation
National meteorological agency heat warning system documentation
Vital registration completeness data from WHO and UN
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic coverage, and documented data-quality limitations — particularly vital registration completeness and heat-specific cause-of-death coding.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering geographic coverage, data quality, temporal consistency, and LMIC data gaps.

title: Map heat early warning system coverage at national and sub-national scale for at least two LMICs
outcome: A heat early warning system coverage map for at least two LMICs, documenting system existence, operational status, population reach, and the distinction between nominal existence and verified operational capacity at sub-national scale.
inputs: WMO heat-health warning system country data
IFRC and national meteorological agency documentation
Published case studies of heat warning system implementation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with coverage methodology and sub-national results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms coverage data extraction, operational status classification, and population-reach estimation methodology for each target country.
done_condition: Coverage map includes methodology, sub-national results, operational status documentation, and replication status for at least two LMICs.

title: Analyze heat-mortality trends in regions with and without warning systems with climate-trend adjustment
outcome: A heat-mortality trend analysis comparing regions with and without warning systems, with explicit documentation of mortality-data quality, climate-trend adjustment methodology, and the distinction between system existence and verified effectiveness.
inputs: heat-mortality data or all-cause mortality with temperature exposure data
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with effectiveness methodology and data-quality documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms mortality-trend methodology, climate-trend adjustment, and data-quality documentation — particularly the distinction between system existence and verified effectiveness.
done_condition: Effectiveness analysis includes methodology, mortality-data quality assessment, climate-trend adjustment, existence-vs-effectiveness distinction, and replication status.

title: Red-team warning-system effectiveness overstatement and confound attribution risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how effectiveness estimates could overstate mortality reduction by confounding with secular healthcare and housing trends, how system-coverage maps could imply protection that does not exist, and how results could be used to justify underinvestment in other climate-adaptation measures.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every effectiveness claim has mortality-data quality documentation, climate-trend adjustment, and confound controls. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every effectiveness claim is either blocked, softened to data-quality-limited language with explicit confound caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match meteorological service and disaster-management decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the coverage-effectiveness analysis would change any real decision for national meteorological services, disaster-management authorities, or climate-adaptation funders without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for meteorological and disaster-management audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that effectiveness estimates are used to justify underinvestment in other adaptation measures or that coverage maps imply protection that does not exist. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Indian Meteorological Department heat-wave coordinator), named decision (e.g., heat action plan resource allocation), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for claiming heat mortality is solved in any LMIC), and replicated field-reality review.
